message:
feat(tui): add detail bar showing full content of selected item (bd-3n1d)

Implements the first option from bd-3n1d: a detail bar below the main
content area that displays untruncated information about the currently
selected worker, active build, or build history entry.

Changes:
- Add show_detail flag and selected_detail() method to TuiState that
  formats full detail text per panel type (workers show all fields,
  active builds show command/worker/progress, history shows
  command/worker/status/exit code)
- Modify render() layout to conditionally include a 3-line detail bar
  between main content and footer when detail is enabled and an item
  is selected
- Add render_detail_bar() widget with "Selected" title border

This eliminates the need for horizontal scrolling since users can now
see the full content of truncated rows by selecting them.

Also updates beads issue tracker (closes bd-37v0 audit E2E, bd-3jsw
in-memory cache, opens bd-3n1d in-progress).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-377q","title":"Idea: Artifact integrity verification (blake3)","description":"## Background
RCH relies on rsync for artifact retrieval; silent corruption is unlikely but high impact. Optional integrity checks provide confidence for critical builds.

## Goals
- Optionally verify key artifacts via blake3 hashes post-transfer.
- Keep disabled by default to avoid extra overhead.

## Design / Approach
- Add config: transfer.verify_artifacts = true/false.
- For known artifact patterns, compute blake3 on worker and locally; compare.
- On mismatch, surface actionable error and fall back to local execution on next run.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add verification routine to transfer pipeline.
- Determine which artifacts to verify (binaries, test reports).
- Extend mock transport to simulate mismatch.

## Tests
- Unit: hash comparison and failure handling.
- Integration: mock mismatch triggers error message.
- E2E: verification disabled by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integrity verification is optional and reliable.
- Clear error messages on mismatch.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-377q.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Skip verification if artifact missing or exceeds size cap.
- Hash on worker before download to avoid duplicate I/O.
- Mismatch error includes both hashes for debugging.

## E2E Outline
- Mock mismatch -> clear error with hashes.
- Verification disabled -> no extra output.
- Successful verification logged with duration.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: hash compute + compare; size cap skip.
- error path includes both hashes and artifact name.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-377q.sh: mock mismatch -> clear error with hashes.
","notes":"## Implementation Complete - Phase 1 (EmeraldMoose)

### Completed:
1. **Config options in TransferConfig** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - `verify_artifacts: bool` (default false)
   - `verify_max_size_bytes: u64` (default 100MB)

2. **Artifact verification module** (rch-common/src/artifact_verify.rs)
   - `FileHash` - blake3 hash + size
   - `ArtifactManifest` - file path -> hash mapping
   - `VerificationResult` - passed/failed/skipped lists
   - `VerificationFailure` - detailed mismatch info
   - `compute_file_hash()` - blake3 computation
   - `verify_artifacts()` - verify against manifest
   - `create_manifest()` - create manifest from files
   - Detailed error formatting with actionable suggestions

3. **9 unit tests** - all passing

### Ready for Use:
The utilities can be used programmatically to verify artifacts. Example:
```rust
use rch_common::{create_manifest, verify_artifacts};

// Create manifest before transfer
let manifest = create_manifest(dir, &file_list, worker_id);

// After transfer, verify
let result = verify_artifacts(dir, &manifest, max_size);
if !result.all_passed() {
    eprintln!("{}", result.format_failures());
}
```

### Future Work (Phase 2 - separate bead):
- [ ] Worker-side blake3 computation via SSH
- [ ] Automatic manifest creation after remote build
- [ ] Integration into retrieve_artifacts pipeline
- [ ] E2E test script

### Verification:
- cargo check passes
- All 9 tests pass
- Config defaults verified","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:53:55.418582543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:19:34.814655713Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:19:34.814523477Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-37hc","title":"Idea: Worker selection audit log","description":"## Background
When selection decisions surprise users, they need a clear explanation of why a worker was chosen or skipped. An audit log makes selection debuggable.

## Goals
- Record per-selection decision inputs and scored outputs.
- Expose via `rch status --workers --verbose` or new `rch selection explain`.
- Keep overhead low and data bounded.

## Design / Approach
- Add structured selection events (scores, weights, reasons).
- Keep ring buffer in daemon with size cap.
- Optionally expose JSON endpoint for tooling.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Extend selection logic to emit decision records.
- Add API + CLI display.
- Add config to cap history size.

## Tests
- Unit: event generation format.
- Integration: CLI shows last decision with reasons.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can understand why a worker was picked.
- No significant perf regressions.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-37hc.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Ring buffer cap with eviction policy.
- Redact sensitive fields (env values, secrets).
- Concurrent selections must not corrupt log ordering.

## E2E Outline
- Run multiple selections -> audit log shows ordered entries.
- JSON endpoint/CLI output includes scores + reasons.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rchd/src/selection.rs: decision log record format + eviction policy.
- rchd/src/api.rs: expose decision log endpoint.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-37hc.sh: run multiple selections and verify ordered audit output.

 {"id":"bd-37o8","title":"Phase 5: Progress & Feedback - Real-time Visual Feedback","description":"Add real-time progress visualization for long-running operations. This phase covers: TransferProgress for rsync operations, CompilationProgress for build status, multi-stage pipeline visualization, spinner animations for indeterminate operations, and completion celebrations for successful builds. The focus is on keeping humans informed during the 10-60+ second operations that are common in compilation workflows.

Design principles:
- Progress output to stderr only (never pollute stdout)
- Use inline updates (carriage return) to avoid log spam
- Degrade gracefully: full bar → simple percentage → dots → nothing (based on capability)
- Support both determinate (known total) and indeterminate (spinner) modes
- Cancel-safe: progress display must clean up on Ctrl+C
- Rate-limit updates to max 10/second to avoid terminal flicker","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:23.453625673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:54:12.411277987Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:54:12.411211914Z","close_reason":"Phase 5 complete: TransferProgress/CompilationProgress/PipelineProgress/spinner/celebration implemented + integrated; tests cover","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37o8","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-37v0","title":"E2E: Integration Test Script for All Audit Fixes","description":"Create a comprehensive end-to-end test script that validates all 4 workstreams and verifies RCH performance requirements from AGENTS.md.

Script location: scripts/audit_e2e.sh (consistent with existing scripts/e2e_test.sh)
Alternatively: tests/audit_integration.rs (Rust integration test)

TEST PLAN:

1. BUILD VERIFICATION:
   - cargo check --all-targets (no compile errors)
   - cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (no warnings)
   - cargo fmt --check (formatting correct)
   - cargo test --all (all unit tests pass)
   - Log: "BUILD: OK" or "BUILD: FAILED at step X"

2. PERFORMANCE BUDGET VERIFICATION (from AGENTS.md lines 250-258):
   - Hook decision for NON-compilation commands: must be <1ms, panic at 5ms
   - Hook decision for compilation commands: must be <5ms, panic at 10ms
   
   Test method:
   - Run classify_command() 100 times on known non-compilation commands (ls, cat, echo, git status)
   - Measure p50, p95, p99 latency
   - FAIL if p99 > 1ms for non-compilation
   
   - Run classify_command() 100 times on known compilation commands (cargo build, make, gcc)
   - Measure p50, p95, p99 latency  
   - FAIL if p99 > 5ms for compilation
   
   Log: "PERF: non-compilation p99={X}ms (<1ms required) {PASS|FAIL}"
   Log: "PERF: compilation p99={X}ms (<5ms required) {PASS|FAIL}"

3. WS1 VERIFICATION (Blocking I/O):
   - Run the hook with a timing history file present
   - Verify spawn_blocking is used (check tokio task metrics if available)
   - Log: timing of load/save operations

4. WS2 VERIFICATION (Allocations):
   - Run classify_command() on 1000 non-compilation commands
   - Measure elapsed time, verify <5ms average
   - Verify reason fields are Cow::Borrowed on reject path (if possible to introspect)
   - Log: "CLASSIFICATION: {N} commands in {T}ms, avg {T/N}ms"

5. WS3 VERIFICATION (Atomics):
   - Create a WorkerState, read all atomic fields concurrently from 10 threads
   - Verify no panics, all values are valid
   - Log: "ATOMIC ACCESS: {N} concurrent reads, 0 errors"

6. WS4 VERIFICATION (Multi-command):
   - Test split_shell_commands with edge cases (quoting, empty, long input)
   - Feed 20 representative multi-command strings through classify_command()
   - Verify expected classification for each
   - Test fail-open: feed malformed input, verify no panic
   - Log each: "MULTI-CMD: '{cmd}' → compilation={bool} (expected={bool}) {PASS|FAIL}"

7. FAIL-OPEN VERIFICATION:
   - Feed intentionally malformed input to the classifier
   - Verify it returns a valid result (not panic/error)
   - Log: "FAIL-OPEN: malformed input handled gracefully {PASS|FAIL}"

8. REGRESSION CHECK:
   - Run full cargo test suite
   - Compare test count before/after (should only increase)
   - Log: "REGRESSION: {passed}/{total} tests passed, {new} new tests added"

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Each section logs [PASS] or [FAIL] with details.
  Include timestamps for each section.
  Final summary: "AUDIT E2E: X/8 sections passed"
  Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 otherwise.

LOGGING:
  Use tracing or simple println with timestamps.
  All output should be parseable for CI integration.
  Follow existing scripts/e2e_test.sh patterns where applicable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:53:49.729766011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:03:11.828858795Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-1gyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-1q0e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-3mog","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37v0","depends_on_id":"bd-3s1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-38kz","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - Infrastructure & Context Detection","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation - Infrastructure & Context Detection

## Overview

This phase establishes the foundational infrastructure for rich_rust integration without changing any existing behavior. By the end of this phase, the codebase will have all necessary modules, types, and utilities in place, but rich output will be opt-in via feature flag (defaulting to OFF during development).

## Why This Phase is Critical

The foundation must be rock-solid because:
1. Every subsequent phase builds on these primitives
2. Context detection determines when rich output is safe to use
3. The theme provides visual consistency across all components
4. The feature flag allows emergency rollback

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Dependency Addition
- Add rich_rust to workspace Cargo.toml
- Add to individual crate Cargo.tomls with appropriate features
- Use path dependency during development, crates.io when published

### 2. UI Module Structure
Create the following directory structure:
```
rch-common/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports and public API
  context.rs   - OutputContext enum and detection
  theme.rs     - RchTheme with color constants
  icons.rs     - Unicode icons with ASCII fallbacks

rch/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports
  console.rs   - RchConsole wrapper

rchd/src/ui/
  mod.rs       - Re-exports
```

### 3. OutputContext Detection
Implement automatic detection of execution context:
- Hook mode: JSON on stdin, should use NO rich output
- Interactive: TTY on stderr, full rich experience
- Colored: No TTY but FORCE_COLOR set, colors only
- Plain: No TTY, no FORCE_COLOR, plain text
- Machine: --json flag or RCH_JSON env var

### 4. RchConsole Wrapper
A context-aware Console that:
- Respects OutputContext settings
- Provides fallback methods (print_or_plain)
- Exposes is_rich() and is_machine() checks

### 5. RchTheme Constants
Define brand colors and semantic styles:
- Primary/Secondary/Accent colors
- Success/Warning/Error/Info semantic colors
- Worker status colors (Healthy/Degraded/Unreachable/etc)
- Text colors (Muted, Dim)

### 6. Feature Flag
- Add 'rich-ui' feature to Cargo.toml
- Default to ON in final release, OFF during development
- Allow compile-time removal of entire rich_rust dependency

## Technical Considerations

### Performance
- Use once_cell::Lazy for static console instances
- Do NOT import UI modules in hook classification path
- Context detection must be <0.1ms

### Compatibility
- Must work on Linux, macOS, Windows
- Must handle terminals without Unicode support
- Must respect NO_COLOR standard (https://no-color.org/)

## Required Unit Tests

### Test File: `rch-common/src/ui/context_tests.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn test_output_context_from_env_detects_hook_mode() {
    // When RCH_HOOK_MODE=1, should return Hook context
    temp_env::with_var("RCH_HOOK_MODE", Some("1"), || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Hook);
    });
}

#[test]
fn test_output_context_respects_no_color() {
    // NO_COLOR environment variable must disable colors
    temp_env::with_var("NO_COLOR", Some("1"), || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert!(!ctx.is_colored());
    });
}

#[test]
fn test_output_context_respects_force_color() {
    // FORCE_COLOR should enable colors even without TTY
    temp_env::with_vars([("FORCE_COLOR", Some("1")), ("NO_COLOR", None)], || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert!(ctx.is_colored());
    });
}

#[test]
fn test_output_context_machine_from_json_flag() {
    // --json flag or RCH_JSON=1 should return Machine context
    temp_env::with_var("RCH_JSON", Some("1"), || {
        let ctx = OutputContext::detect();
        assert_eq!(ctx, OutputContext::Machine);
    });
}
```

### Test File: `rch-common/src/ui/theme_tests.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn test_rch_theme_colors_are_valid() {
    let theme = RchTheme::default();
    // All colors should be valid Color enums
    assert!(theme.primary().is_some());
    assert!(theme.success().is_some());
    assert!(theme.error().is_some());
}

#[test]
fn test_worker_status_colors_are_distinct() {
    let theme = RchTheme::default();
    // Status colors should be visually distinct
    assert_ne!(theme.worker_healthy(), theme.worker_degraded());
    assert_ne!(theme.worker_degraded(), theme.worker_unreachable());
}
```

### Test File: `rch-common/src/ui/icons_tests.rs`

```rust
#[test]
fn test_icons_have_ascii_fallbacks() {
    let icons = Icons::new(false); // ASCII mode
    // All icons should have ASCII fallbacks
    assert!(!icons.success().is_empty());
    assert!(!icons.error().is_empty());
    assert!(!icons.warning().is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn test_icons_unicode_vs_ascii_different() {
    let unicode = Icons::new(true);
    let ascii = Icons::new(false);
    // Unicode icons should differ from ASCII
    assert_ne!(unicode.success(), ascii.success());
}
```

### Minimum Test Count: 12 tests
- OutputContext detection: 4 tests
- RchTheme: 3 tests
- Icons: 2 tests
- Integration: 3 tests

## E2E Test Script

### `scripts/e2e_ui_foundation.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E Test: UI Foundation Infrastructure
# Tests that the UI module correctly detects context and respects env vars

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
LOG_FILE="/tmp/rch_e2e_ui_foundation_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_pass() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] PASS $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_fail() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] FAIL $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; exit 1; }

log "Starting UI Foundation E2E tests"
log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"

# Build
log "Building rch..."
cargo build -p rch --release 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
RCH="$PROJECT_ROOT/target/release/rch"

# Test 1: NO_COLOR disables ANSI
log "Test 1: NO_COLOR environment variable"
OUTPUT=$(NO_COLOR=1 $RCH status 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes present despite NO_COLOR=1"
fi
log_pass "NO_COLOR correctly disables ANSI codes"

# Test 2: Hook mode produces no rich output
log "Test 2: Hook mode JSON purity"
HOOK_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}'
HOOK_OUTPUT=$(echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | RCH_HOOK_MODE=1 $RCH hook --stdin 2>/dev/null)
if ! echo "$HOOK_OUTPUT" | jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_fail "Hook output is not valid JSON"
fi
if echo "$HOOK_OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes in hook JSON output"
fi
log_pass "Hook mode produces clean JSON"

# Test 3: --json flag produces machine output
log "Test 3: --json flag machine output"
JSON_OUTPUT=$($RCH status --json 2>/dev/null || true)
if echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\['; then
    log_fail "ANSI codes in --json output"
fi
if ! echo "$JSON_OUTPUT" | jq -e . >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_fail "--json output is not valid JSON"
fi
log_pass "--json produces clean machine output"

# Test 4: Feature flag compilation
log "Test 4: rich-ui feature flag"
cargo build -p rch --release --no-default-features 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
log_pass "Builds without rich-ui feature"

log "========================================"
log "All UI Foundation E2E tests passed!"
log "Full log: $LOG_FILE"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] rich_rust dependency added to workspace
- [ ] UI module structure created in rch-common, rch, rchd
- [ ] OutputContext enum with 5 variants implemented
- [ ] OutputContext::detect() works correctly for all environments
- [ ] RchTheme with all color constants defined
- [ ] Icons with Unicode and ASCII fallbacks
- [ ] 'rich-ui' feature flag compiles without errors
- [ ] NO_COLOR and FORCE_COLOR environment variables respected
- [ ] Unit tests pass: 12+ tests
- [ ] E2E test script passes: `scripts/e2e_ui_foundation.sh`
- [ ] Zero performance impact on hook classification path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:32.925019783Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:30:15.299666060Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:30:15.299518435Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38kz","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-38td","title":"Add TUI keyboard shortcuts for worker actions","description":"# Add Worker Action Keyboard Shortcuts

## Problem
Users must leave TUI to drain/enable workers or cancel builds. The TUI is read-only when it could support common actions.

## Proposed Shortcuts
When Workers panel is selected:
```
d     Drain selected worker (with confirmation)
e     Enable selected worker  
D     Drain ALL workers (with confirmation)
E     Enable ALL workers
r     Refresh worker status immediately
p     Probe selected worker
```

When Active Builds panel is selected:
```
k     Cancel selected build (SIGTERM, with confirmation)
K     Force kill selected build (SIGKILL)
c     Copy build command to clipboard
```

When Build History panel is selected:
```
c     Copy build details to clipboard
Enter View build logs (already works)
/     Filter builds (already works, but undocumented)
```

## Implementation Notes
1. Drain/enable should call daemon API via Unix socket
2. Cancel should send signal to remote process
3. Confirmations should use a modal dialog
4. Actions should show success/error feedback

## API Calls Needed
- POST /workers/{id}/drain
- POST /workers/{id}/enable
- POST /builds/{id}/cancel
- POST /builds/{id}/kill

These APIs should already exist in rchd; we just need to call them from TUI.

## Confirmation Dialog
For destructive actions, show centered modal:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│ Drain worker 'css'?             │
│                                 │
│ This will stop routing new      │
│ jobs to this worker.            │
│                                 │
│        [Y]es    [N]o            │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/app.rs (event handling, API calls)
- rch/src/tui/state.rs (action state tracking)
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs (confirmation dialog rendering, help text)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:59.226925880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:02:48.705933353Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:02:48.705869114Z","close_reason":"Added worker/build action shortcuts (D/E drain/enable all, x/X cancel/force-kill build) with confirmation dialog modal. Added cancel_build/force_kill_build daemon APIs. 10 new event tests, 7 new state tests, 4 new widget tests. All 1582 tests pass, clippy clean. Used x/X instead of k/K for cancel to avoid vim navigation conflict.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38td","depends_on_id":"bd-1olr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38td","depends_on_id":"bd-ryot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:47Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-38td","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rch/src/tui/app_test.rs)
1. `test_d_drains_selected_worker()`
2. `test_e_enables_selected_worker()`
3. `test_k_cancels_selected_build()`
4. `test_K_force_kills_build()`
5. `test_c_copies_to_clipboard()`
6. `test_confirmation_modal_renders()`
7. `test_confirmation_y_confirms()`
8. `test_confirmation_n_cancels()`

### Integration Tests
1. Drain worker via TUI, verify state change
2. Cancel build via TUI, verify build stops
3. Verify clipboard integration works

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_tui_actions.sh)
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_tui_actions_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Testing TUI worker actions"

# Test 1: Drain via TUI
log "Test 1: Drain worker via TUI"
BEFORE_STATE=$(rch workers list --json | jq -r '.[0].status')
# Simulate: open TUI, select first worker, press d, confirm
# (would use expect or test mode with key injection)

# Test 2: Enable via TUI
log "Test 2: Enable worker via TUI"

# Test 3: Confirmation modal
log "Test 3: Verify confirmation appears for destructive actions"
rch dashboard --test-mode --inject-key d --dump-state 2>&1 | grep -q "confirm" || {
  log "FAIL: No confirmation for drain"
  exit 1
}

log "TUI action tests complete"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log action request at INFO level
- Log confirmation response at DEBUG level
- Log API call result at DEBUG level
- Log errors at ERROR level with context","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3i0q","title":"Idea: Project-local .rchignore excludes","description":"## Background
Projects often need custom excludes beyond global config (large datasets, generated artifacts, secrets). A project-local ignore file aligns with familiar workflows and reduces transfer size/risk.

## Goals
- Support `.rchignore` in project root with gitignore-style globs.
- Merge with existing transfer exclude patterns (config + defaults).
- Keep behavior deterministic and visible in verbose logs.

## Design / Approach
- Read `.rchignore` if present; parse line-based patterns (ignore comments/blank lines).
- Merge into transfer_config.exclude_patterns with stable ordering.
- Provide `rch diagnose` / verbose hook logging to show effective excludes count.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add parser for `.rchignore` lines.
- Integrate into transfer pipeline and mock rsync layer.
- Update docs (README + config guide) with examples and precedence.

## Tests
- Unit: parse behavior (comments, blank lines, whitespace).
- Integration: `.rchignore` overrides applied to rsync call (mock).
- E2E: excluding a large fixture reduces transfer size.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `.rchignore` works without config changes.
- Effective excludes are visible in verbose mode.
- No regression in default exclude handling.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-3i0q.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Ignore comments/blank lines; trim whitespace.
- No full gitignore negation support (document that `!` is literal for now).
- Merge order is deterministic: defaults + config + .rchignore.

## E2E Outline
- Add large file + .rchignore pattern -> rsync excludes it.
- Remove .rchignore -> file is transferred again.
- Verbose output shows effective exclude count.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: .rchignore parser handles comments/blank lines/whitespace.
- rch/src/transfer.rs: merge order deterministic.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-3i0q.sh: create large file, ignore it, verify rsync excludes.
- Remove .rchignore and verify file transfers again.

 {"id":"bd-3ib0","title":"Unify worker state terminology across CLI","description":"# Unify Worker State Terminology

## Problem
The CLI uses confusing, overlapping terms for worker states:

### Current Commands
- `rch workers drain css` - Stops sending new jobs, waits for current to finish
- `rch workers disable css` - Also stops sending jobs? How different from drain?
- `rch workers enable css` - Resumes sending jobs

### User Confusion
Users don't understand:
1. What's the difference between drain and disable?
2. Is 'paused' the same as 'drained'?
3. Can a disabled worker be drained?
4. What's the opposite of drain - 'undrain' or 'enable'?

## Proposed Terminology

### Worker States (clear hierarchy)
```
ENABLED (normal operation)
  ↓ rch workers drain
DRAINING (accepting no new jobs, finishing current)
  ↓ (all jobs complete)
DRAINED (idle, no jobs, ready to disable)
  ↓ rch workers disable
DISABLED (completely offline, skipped by selection)
```

### Commands
```
rch workers drain <id>     Start graceful drain
rch workers enable <id>    Resume from drained/draining
rch workers disable <id>   Mark worker as offline (must be drained first)
rch workers status <id>    Show detailed worker state
```

### Remove/Deprecate
- Remove 'pause' terminology entirely
- Deprecate direct 'disable' without drain (or require --force)

## Documentation Updates
- Update --help text for all worker commands
- Add state diagram to rch workers --help
- Update README.md worker management section

## Migration
1. Add deprecation warning to confusing usages
2. Keep old behavior for 1-2 releases
3. Remove deprecated paths

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/main.rs (command definitions, help text)
- rch/src/commands.rs (worker state handling)
- rch/src/status_display.rs (status output)
- README.md (documentation)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:06:08.311137466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:46:46.819516487Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:46:46.819450474Z","close_reason":"Added Drained state to WorkerStatus enum with automatic transition from Draining when all jobs complete. Updated theme, UI, API, health, alerts, telemetry, workers, and selection to handle new state. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3jru","title":"Implement MetricsDashboard for periodic stats display","description":"Create MetricsDashboard in rchd/src/ui/metrics.rs for performance summary:
- Periodic statistics panel (configurable interval, default 5min)
- Jobs completed, failed, cache hits in period
- Average job duration with trend indicator (↑↓→)
- Worker utilization percentages
- Transfer volume (bytes synced up/down)

Technical requirements:
- Only display when stderr is TTY and rich output enabled
- Use Table for metrics with right-aligned numbers
- Include sparkline-style trend indicators using Unicode blocks
- Calculate and display percentiles (p50, p95, p99) for latency
- Reset counters option with --metrics-reset-interval
- Support Prometheus-compatible metric names in description

Example periodic output:
╭─ Metrics (last 5 minutes) ───────────────────╮
│ Jobs: 47 completed, 2 failed (96% success)   │
│ Cache: 38 hits, 9 misses (81% hit rate)      │
│ Avg Duration: 23.4s (↓ improving)            │
│ Workers: 89% utilized │ Queue peak: 12 jobs  │
│ Transfer: ↑ 1.2 GB  ↓ 3.4 GB                 │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────╯","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:56.185396572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:46:04.682633418Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:46:04.682566413Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jru","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jru","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3jsw","title":"WS1.5: (Optional) Add in-memory cache for TimingHistory","description":"FOLLOW-UP OPTIMIZATION after WS1.1-WS1.4 are complete.

PROBLEM: spawn_blocking moves I/O off the async thread but doesn't reduce latency. If the timing history file is slow to read (HDD, cold cache, large file), the decision path can still exceed the 5ms budget.

SOLUTION: Keep TimingHistory in memory with write-through to disk.

Implementation:
1. Add a static or process-global cache: OnceCell<RwLock<TimingHistory>>
2. On first access (lazy load): spawn_blocking(load) and store in cache
3. estimate_timing_for_build: Read from cache (no disk I/O)
4. record_build_timing: Update cache AND spawn_blocking(save) for persistence
5. Process restart: Cache is cold, first access loads from disk

Benefits:
- Decision path has zero disk I/O after first load
- Writes are async and don't block decisions
- Survives process restart (data persisted to disk)

Risks:
- Cache stale if file is edited externally (acceptable for this use case)
- Memory usage (timing history is small, ~KB)

This is P3 because spawn_blocking in WS1.1-WS1.2 is sufficient for most cases. This optimization is for high-throughput scenarios.

 {"id":"bd-3knb","title":"Add rich_rust dependency to workspace Cargo.toml","description":"# Add rich_rust dependency to workspace Cargo.toml

## Task Description

Add rich_rust as a workspace dependency in the root Cargo.toml, then add it to each relevant crate's Cargo.toml (rch, rchd, rch-common).

## Background

rich_rust is located at /dp/rich_rust during development. It provides:
- Console struct for terminal output
- Style and Color types for styling
- Table, Panel, Tree, ProgressBar renderables
- Markup parsing ([bold red]text[/])
- Automatic terminal capability detection

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add to workspace Cargo.toml

```toml
[workspace.dependencies]
# During development, use path:
rich_rust = { path = "/dp/rich_rust", features = ["full"] }

# When published, switch to:
# rich_rust = { version = "0.1", features = ["full"] }
```

### 2. Add to rch/Cargo.toml

```toml
[dependencies]
rich_rust = { workspace = true, optional = true }

[features]
default = ["rich-ui"]
rich-ui = ["rich_rust"]
```

### 3. Add to rchd/Cargo.toml

Same as rch/Cargo.toml.

### 4. Add to rch-common/Cargo.toml

```toml
[dependencies]
# Minimal features for shared types
rich_rust = { workspace = true, optional = true, default-features = false }

[features]
default = ["rich-ui"]
rich-ui = ["rich_rust"]
```

## Technical Notes

### Why "full" features?
The full feature set includes:
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON pretty-printing (serde_json)

We want all of these for maximum capability.

### Why optional?
Making rich_rust optional allows:
1. Compile-time removal if issues arise
2. Smaller binaries for users who dont need rich output
3. Faster compilation without feature

### Path vs Published
During development, use path dependency for rapid iteration.
Switch to crates.io version when rich_rust is published.

## Verification

```bash
# Should compile successfully
cargo check --all-targets

# Should compile without rich-ui
cargo check --all-targets --no-default-features

# Should compile with rich-ui
cargo check --all-targets --features rich-ui
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] Workspace Cargo.toml has rich_rust in [workspace.dependencies]
2. [ ] rch/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with optional + feature flag
3. [ ] rchd/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with optional + feature flag
4. [ ] rch-common/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with minimal features
5. [ ] cargo check passes with default features
6. [ ] cargo check passes with --no-default-features","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:51.267049982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:33:17.865319328Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:33:17.865271618Z","close_reason":"Added rich_rust as workspace dependency with optional feature flags in rch, rchd, and rch-common. Fixed unicode-width version conflict and clippy warnings in selection.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3knb","depends_on_id":"bd-38kz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mog","title":"WS2.4: Unit tests for zero-allocation reject path","description":"Add tests that verify zero heap allocations on the reject path:

1. Classification correctness tests:
   - Test all existing test cases still pass after Cow conversion
   - Specifically test that known non-compilation commands (ls, cat, echo, git status) produce correct Classification with Cow::Borrowed reason
   - Test that compilation commands (cargo build, make, gcc) produce correct Classification

2. Allocation-free reject path verification:
   - Use a custom allocator counter (or #[global_allocator] with counting) to verify that classify_command("ls") produces zero heap allocations
   - Alternative: use std::hint::black_box + criterion benchmark showing 0 allocs
   - At minimum: verify the reason field is Cow::Borrowed (not Cow::Owned) for reject cases

3. Cow roundtrip tests:
   - Verify Classification.reason can be displayed, compared, serialized/deserialized
   - Verify ClassificationTier.name and .reason work with serde (Cow<str> serializes as string)

Test location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (inline #[cfg(test)] module)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:52:50.341239385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T03:45:54.605195044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T03:45:54.605103644Z","close_reason":"Added 17 unit tests verifying zero-allocation Cow reject path, serde roundtrips, and correctness after Cow conversion.  {"id":"bd-3n00","title":"Create Unit Tests for Test Harness & Utilities","description":"## Purpose
Create comprehensive unit tests for the test harness itself, ensuring the test infrastructure is reliable before using it to test rch.

## Why This Matters
- A buggy test harness produces false positives/negatives
- Harness bugs are harder to debug than rch bugs
- Unit tests catch harness issues before they affect e2e tests
- Provides documentation of expected harness behavior

## Test Categories

### 1. Logging Infrastructure Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/logging_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_log_entry_is_valid_json() {
    let logger = TestLogger::new("test_example");
    logger.info("test message", &[("key", "value")]);
    let log_content = std::fs::read_to_string(logger.log_path()).unwrap();
    for line in log_content.lines() {
        serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line).expect("invalid JSON");
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_timing_accuracy() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_log_levels_filter_correctly() { ... }
```

### 2. Output Comparison Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/output_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_identical_outputs_compare_equal() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_path_normalization() {
    let local = "/home/user/project/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let remote = "/tmp/rch/project_abc/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let normalizer = OutputNormalizer::default();
    assert_eq!(normalizer.normalize(local), normalizer.normalize(remote));
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_shows_actual_differences() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_binary_comparison_exact() { ... }
```

### 3. Worker Discovery Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/discovery_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_workers_available_returns_false_when_no_config() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_skip_mechanism_logs_reason() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_health_check_timeout() { ... }
```

### 4. Harness Lifecycle Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/harness_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_daemon_spawn_and_cleanup() {
    let harness = TrueE2EHarness::new_or_skip().unwrap();
    // Daemon should be running
    assert!(harness.daemon_is_healthy());
    drop(harness);
    // Daemon should be cleaned up (if harness spawned it)
}

#[test]
fn test_fixture_isolation() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_project_cleanup() { ... }
```

### 5. Timing Utilities Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/timing_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_measure_command_timing_accuracy() {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let measured = measure_command_timing("sleep 0.1");
    let actual = start.elapsed();
    // Should be within 10ms of actual
    assert!((measured.as_millis() as i64 - actual.as_millis() as i64).abs() < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_percentile_calculation() { ... }
```

-{"id":"bd-3n1d","title":"Add TUI horizontal scroll for long text content","description":"# Add Horizontal Scroll for Long Text

## Problem
Long worker IDs, hostnames, or build commands get truncated in TUI panels. Users can't see full content.

## Examples
```
┌─ Workers ─────────────────────────────┐
│ ● production-us-east-1-buil...  (4/8) │  ← Truncated\!
│ ● production-us-west-2-buil...  (2/8) │
│ ● staging-worker-with-very-...  (0/8) │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Proposed Solution

### Option 1: Scroll on Selection
When a row is selected, show full content in a status bar:
```
┌─ Workers ─────────────────────────────┐
│ ● production-us-east-1-build-srv (4/8)│
│ ● production-us-west-2-buil...  (2/8) │
├───────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Selected: production-us-east-1-build-server.internal.company.com │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Option 2: Horizontal Scroll Keys
Left/Right arrows scroll the content:
```
← h/→ l: scroll | ↑ k/↓ j: select
...duction-us-east-1-build-server.int...
```

### Recommendation
Implement Option 1 (status bar) first - simpler and more discoverable.

## Implementation
1. Add detail_line field to TuiState for selected item info
2. When selection changes, update detail_line
3. Render detail_line in a status bar below the panel

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/state.rs (add detail_line field)
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs (render detail bar)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:13:44.221313199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:13:44.221313199Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3ncy","title":"Add confirmation prompts to destructive CLI commands","description":"# Add Confirmation Prompts to Destructive Commands

## Problem
Several commands take immediate action without confirmation, which can disrupt active work:

### rch daemon restart (main.rs line 678)
- Restarts daemon immediately
- Interrupts all active SSH connections
- Can cause in-progress builds to fail
- No warning, no confirmation

### rch workers drain
- Immediately stops routing to worker
- No confirmation for single worker
- --all has --yes flag but single worker doesn't

## Commands Needing Confirmation

| Command | Risk | Current | Proposed |
|---------|------|---------|----------|
| rch daemon restart | Interrupts builds | None | Confirm if active builds |
| rch daemon stop | Interrupts builds | None | Confirm if active builds |
| rch workers drain | Stops routing | None (single) | Always confirm |
| rch fleet drain --all | Stops all routing | --yes flag | Keep as-is |
| rch cancel --all | Kills all builds | --yes flag | Keep as-is |

## Implementation Pattern
```rust
async fn daemon_restart(force: bool) -> Result<()> {
    let status = get_daemon_status().await?;
    
    if \!force && status.active_jobs > 0 {
        eprintln\!("Warning: {} active jobs will be interrupted.", status.active_jobs);
        if \!confirm("Restart daemon?")? {
            return Ok(());
        }
    }
    
    // Proceed with restart
}
```

## Bypass for Automation
All confirmation prompts should respect:
- --yes / -y flag to skip confirmation
- Non-interactive mode (no TTY) should require --yes

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/commands.rs (add confirmation logic)
- rch/src/main.rs (add --yes flag to restart/stop if missing)

## Testing
- Verify prompts appear for destructive actions
- Verify --yes bypasses prompts
- Verify non-TTY fails without --yes
- Verify JSON output mode works correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:45.231015279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T19:10:13.977251596Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T19:10:13.976768647Z","close_reason":"Implemented confirmation prompts for daemon stop/restart in commit 148efa4","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ncy","depends_on_id":"bd-2u2g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-3ncy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rch/src/commands_test.rs)
1. `test_daemon_restart_prompts_when_active_builds()`
2. `test_daemon_restart_skips_prompt_with_yes_flag()`
3. `test_daemon_restart_skips_prompt_when_no_active()`
4. `test_workers_drain_prompts_for_confirmation()`
5. `test_confirmation_denied_aborts_action()`
6. `test_non_tty_requires_yes_flag()`

### Integration Tests
1. Start active build, try restart, verify prompt appears
2. Run with --yes, verify no prompt
3. Run without TTY, verify error without --yes

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_confirmations.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_confirm_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

# Test 1: daemon restart prompts
log "Test 1: daemon restart should prompt with active builds"
# Start a slow build
rch self-test --worker css &
BUILD_PID=$!
sleep 2

# Try restart without --yes, should prompt (and fail in non-TTY)
if echo "n" | rch daemon restart 2>&1 | grep -qi "restart"; then
  log "PASS: Restart prompts for confirmation"
else
  log "WARN: Could not verify prompt (may be non-TTY mode)"
fi

kill $BUILD_PID 2>/dev/null || true

# Test 2: --yes bypasses prompt
log "Test 2: --yes bypasses confirmation"
rch daemon restart --yes 2>&1 | grep -qi "restart" || {
  log "PASS: --yes bypassed confirmation"
}

# Test 3: Non-TTY requires --yes
log "Test 3: Non-TTY without --yes should fail"
if echo "" | rch daemon restart 2>&1 | grep -qi "requires.*--yes"; then
  log "PASS: Non-TTY correctly requires --yes"
fi

log "Confirmation tests complete"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log confirmation prompt decision at INFO level
- Log user response at DEBUG level
- Log bypass via --yes at DEBUG level","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ndq","title":"Implement JobLifecycleLog for structured job event display","description":"Create JobLifecycleLog formatter in rchd/src/ui/jobs.rs for job event visualization:
- Job submission: job ID, source, command summary, assigned worker
- Job progress: compilation phase, elapsed time, resource usage
- Job completion: success/failure indicator, duration, artifact count
- Job failure: error type, worker state, suggested remediation

Technical requirements:
- Prefix each line with timestamp and job ID for log grep-ability
- Use consistent column alignment for scanability
- Color code by event type: blue (start), yellow (progress), green (success), red (failure)
- Include Unicode status icons inline
- Rate-limit progress updates (max 1/second) to prevent log spam
- Support both single-line compact and multi-line detailed modes

Format examples:
[14:30:01] [j-a3f2] ● START cargo build --release → worker1
[14:30:15] [j-a3f2] ◐ BUILD 14.0s │ Compiling foo v0.1.0 (45/120 crates)
[14:30:45] [j-a3f2] ✓ DONE  44.2s │ 156 artifacts │ cache written
[14:30:46] [j-b7c1] ✗ FAIL  12.1s │ worker2 timeout │ retry queued","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoReef","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:49.774732595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:53:59.335814886Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:53:59.335719078Z","close_reason":"Implemented JobLifecycleLog formatter in rchd/src/ui/jobs.rs (compact/detailed, colors/icons, per-job rate limiting) with unit tests; verified via cargo test -p rchd jobs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ndq","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ndq","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
